title: Direct behavior review includes assertion deltas in existing named tests
context: The automated G2 loop stalled at 9/10 exact P3 policies because direct review only recognized newly named tests. Human-confirmed fixes often strengthen an existing named regression, leaving their strongest executable evidence invisible.
decision: For an exact current human-confirmed fixing decision, direct behavior review also emits a candidate when a literal node:test test()/it() name exists on both sides and the fixing diff adds a line inside that exact parsed test call. Candidate identity binds the decision, fixing commit, file, test name, full known-good source hash, and modified-test grounding. Replay and human disposition rules remain unchanged.
consequences: Strengthened existing regressions can participate in the same exact fail-to-pass replay and P3 materialization loop as newly named tests.
Comment-only or non-regressing changes can become review candidates but remain non-authoritative and will be rejected by pass/pass replay.
Legacy added-test candidate identities and receipts remain byte-stable.
alternatives_rejected: Require a new test name for every fix: rejected because it blocks valid regression-test strengthening and incentivizes test churn.
Treat any modified test file as one candidate: rejected because it is too broad to bind a selected test behavior.
Select helper symbols or assertion text structurally: rejected because implementation details are neither necessary nor sufficient to prove behavior.
